headline: BofA and UBS Cut Nu Holdings (NU) Price Targets
summary: Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) is one of the 12 Best Revenue Growth Stocks to Buy According to Wall Street Analysts. On May 21, BofA reduced its price target on Nu Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NU) from $17 to $16 and kept its Neutral rating on the stock after the company reported its results for Q1 2026. The […]

headline: JPM, WFC & Others Clear Key Regulatory Hurdle on Living Wills

headline: How The Schrödinger (SDGR) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analysts Reset Price Targets
summary: Schrödinger’s fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly from US$21.38 to US$20.88, bringing it in line with the updated implied price target in the model. This mirrors recent Street research, where several firms have also cut price targets while stressing that the shift reflects refreshed views on valuation, revenue recognition and execution risks rather than a single catalyst. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets shape the evolving analyst narrative and what to watch...

headline: How The Choice Hotels (CHH) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Valuation Assumptions
summary: Choice Hotels International has seen its modeled fair value trimmed slightly to US$112.53 from US$114.13, signaling a small reset in where some analysts think the stock could reasonably trade. This shift sits alongside a mix of higher and lower price targets across the Street, as bullish and bearish analysts weigh growth potential against concerns tied to sector risks and company execution. As you read on, you will see how to track these evolving calls and what they might mean for your own...

headline: BofA’s Vivek Arya Sees Nvidia at $350 as Agentic AI Drives an “Unprecedented” Chip Cycle
summary: Bank of America’s Vivek Arya walked onto CNBC with a number that sits well above consensus. His price target on NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) is $350, against a stock trading around $215. Arya is making a longer call, and this might lead to other analysts also revising their estimates upwards in the coming days and weeks. From ... headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as Fed Chair With Lowest Senate Votes Ever Amid Independence Concerns
summary: Kevin Warsh takes the oath as Federal Reserve chair on Friday in a White House ceremony, only the second Fed swearing-in held at the executive mansion in nearly 40 years. The last was Ronald Reagan hosting Alan Greenspan in 1987, a tableau the Trump White House is happy to revive. The optics matter because Warsh ... headline: Is the Stock Market Open Today? Here Are the Trading Hours for Memorial Day.
summary: As millions of Americans hit the road for Memorial Day weekend, investors are welcoming the chance to slow down.  The holiday follows a chaotic week driven by quarterly earnings from artificial-intelligence giant  Nvidia  and a flurry of excitement around quantum computing stocks.  Is the Stock Market Open or Closed on Memorial Day?
